channel.title: Allow passing key and cert to use with https when running frontity dev
channel.description:
<!-- Please, follow this template to be as efficient as possible. Before opening a new feature, please check that it hasn't been already added. -->
# Description <!-- Try to give a detailed explanation of what do you expect of this feature --> Just dropping this idea here for the future. I think that the `frontity dev` command should allow the developer to pass a key and a certificate to be used when loading the site over `https`.
# Examples <!-- Do you know examples that could inspire the community? Let us know! -->
``` npm run dev --https --key localhost-key.pem --cert localhost.pem ``` # Possible solution <!-- If you have already thought of a possible solution, let us know! --> Implement the CLI options from the example above.

item.title: Allow passing key and cert to use with https when running frontity dev
item.dc:creator: orballo
item.description:
Description

Just dropping this idea here for the future. I think that the frontity dev command should allow the developer to pass a key and a certificate to be used when loading the site over https.

Examples

npm run dev --https --key localhost-key.pem --cert localhost.pem

Possible solution

Implement the CLI options from the example above.

Read full topic
item.source: Allow passing key and cert to use with https when running frontity dev
